message:
fix(websocket): Reject invalid frames per RFC 6455 protocol requirements

Add validation for WebSocket protocol violations that were previously
silently accepted, improving security and interoperability:

1. RSV bits validation: Reject frames with RSV1/2/3 set when no
   extensions are negotiated (prevents extension confusion attacks)

2. Control frame validation: Reject fragmented control frames and
   control frames with payload > 125 bytes (protocol violations)

3. Reorder frame parsing to validate control frame constraints
   before reading the mask key (fail-fast on invalid frames)

Add comprehensive tests for each rejection case to prevent regressions:
- test_reader_rejects_control_frame_over_125
- test_reader_rejects_fragmented_control_frame
- test_reader_rejects_rsv_bits

These checks align with RFC 6455 sections 5.2 (RSV) and 5.5 (control
frames) and improve compatibility with strict WebSocket implementations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
